title: Representation Class Falsification
purpose: Test whether any estimator built on the structured fingerprint representation class produces separation from B1 that is invariant under the C-5 perturbation battery. Test 4 pre-declared candidate decision rules and combine per-rule verdicts into a class-level answer to RQ-C6.
research_question: RQ-C6: Can any estimator built on the structured fingerprint representation class produce separation from B1 that is invariant under the distribution transformations tested in C-5?

R1_C_genuine.rule_verbatim: ACCEPT if obs_fails == 0; ACCEPT if obs_fails > 0 AND all failures share one probe_family; REJECT otherwise.
R1_C_genuine.rule_pseudocode: if obs_records is None: return 'ACCEPT' if obs_fails == 0 else 'REJECT'; failures = [r for r in obs_records if not r['pass_fail']]; if len(failures) == 0: return 'ACCEPT'; families = {r['fingerprint_context']['probe_family'] for r in failures}; if len(families) == 1: return 'ACCEPT'; return 'REJECT'
R1_C_genuine.fallback_when_obs_records_none: ACCEPT if obs_fails == 0, REJECT otherwise (B1 behavior)

R2_C_feature_threshold.rule_verbatim: ACCEPT if obs_fails == 0; ACCEPT if failure_rate_on_deformed_probes < 0.5; REJECT otherwise. A 'deformed' probe has deformation_level > 0. If no deformed probes, fall back to B1 behavior.
R2_C_feature_threshold.rule_pseudocode: if obs_records is None: return 'ACCEPT' if obs_fails == 0 else 'REJECT'; deformed = [r for r in obs_records if r['fingerprint_context'].get('deformation_level', 0) > 0]; if len(deformed) == 0: return 'ACCEPT' if obs_fails == 0 else 'REJECT'; failures_deformed = [r for r in deformed if not r['pass_fail']]; rate = len(failures_deformed) / len(deformed); if obs_fails == 0: return 'ACCEPT'; if rate < 0.5: return 'ACCEPT'; return 'REJECT'
R2_C_feature_threshold.fallback_when_no_deformed_probes: B1 behavior (ACCEPT if obs_fails == 0, REJECT otherwise)

R3_C_majority.rule_verbatim: ACCEPT if obs_fails == 0; ACCEPT if there is a unique mode in the failure-family distribution (one family has more failures than any other single family); REJECT otherwise. Ties yield REJECT.
R3_C_majority.rule_pseudocode: if obs_records is None: return 'ACCEPT' if obs_fails == 0 else 'REJECT'; failures = [r for r in obs_records if not r['pass_fail']]; if len(failures) == 0: return 'ACCEPT'; counts = {}; for r in failures: f = r['fingerprint_context']['probe_family']; counts[f] = counts.get(f, 0) + 1; max_count = max(counts.values()); n_with_max = sum(1 for c in counts.values() if c == max_count); if n_with_max == 1: return 'ACCEPT'; return 'REJECT'

R4_C_zero_only.role: negative control: shows whether C-4 gain is associated with coherence condition (R1) or with being more permissive than B1
R4_C_zero_only.expected_verdict: DOES_NOT_SURVIVE a priori (gap = 0 on every perturbation)

per_rule.SURVIVES: gap > 0.10 on all 11 perturbations at all 9 lambda values, on LC322, with aggregate-consistency check passing
per_rule.DOES_NOT_SURVIVE: any perturbation has gap <= 0.10 or gap < 0 at any lambda value
per_rule.DEGENERATE: rule or B1 collapses to all-ACCEPT or all-REJECT at a given lambda; record and exclude at that lambda; degenerate on all lambdas -> DOES_NOT_SURVIVE

rq_c6_class_level.YES: at least one of R1, R2, R3 SURVIVES the C-5 battery on LC322. The class can produce invariant separation.
rq_c6_class_level.NO: none of R1, R2, R3 SURVIVES. The class does not produce invariant separation under the tested perturbations.
rq_c6_class_level.MIXED: some rules survive and some do not, no rule is a refinement of another. Per-rule status reported; class contains both surviving and non-surviving rules.

falsification_criterion.lc45_role: stress_test only; LC45 result reported per rule separately; does not override LC322 verdict on RQ-C6

aggregate_consistency_check.check: Re-run B1 on unperturbed LC322 (and LC45); recomputed (WA, WR) must equal stored (WA, WR) in data/midweather_fingerprint_lc{322,45}.json
aggregate_consistency_check.on_failure: STOP IMMEDIATELY. Do not record any result. Surface discrepancy to Foued.
